subject: DevOps
title: Static Application Security Testing (SAST)
description: A quick win: understand Static Application Security Testing (SAST).

hook.text: The scanner found a SQL injection on line 42. The dev fixed it before the code left their laptop.

buildup.text: SAST scans source code for flaws without running the app. It catches issues at the earliest moment.

discovery.text: SonarQube, Semgrep, and Checkmarx analyze code patterns and flag insecure constructs automatically.

twist.text: SAST has false positives. Not every flag is exploitable. Developers must triage results carefully.

climax.text: Add SAST to CI/CD. Every PR gets scanned. Security issues block merge, just like failing tests.

punchline.text: Scan code before it runs. SAST catches flaws at the source.

quiz.question: When does SAST analyze code for vulnerabilities?
options: Before the application runs, by scanning source code
Only after the application is deployed to production
During runtime by monitoring network traffic
